title: Site Plan -- West Campus Renovation, East Campus Demolition, and 393-Unit One-for-One Replacement
summary: Developers present full site plan: 393 total apartments split between west campus (200 units, outlined for renovation) and east campus (193 units, slated for demolition and replacement with 2 new buildings -- Building A: 5 stories at former Patrician/Snowflakes Bakery site, Building B: 4 stories at former deli site). East campus identified as unsalvageable due to: super-block topography preventing emergency vehicle access, multiple unsecured entrances/exits, basement tunnels connecting buildings enabling criminal escape, hilly terrain hard to control, mid-century 'poor planning' design. Camera vandalism, door destruction, and squatter flooding documented as ongoing challenges. West campus renovations: all-new kitchens (quartz counters, wood cabinets), bathrooms, appliances, plumbing/electrical systems, roofs, windows, facade, landscaping, playgrounds, and electric heat pump HVAC replacing baseboard. Renovation and new construction simultaneous; financing expected March-June 2026 from HCR. Commissioner Michael Collins (Neighborhood and Business Development) explains HUD requires maintaining unit count -- east campus residents must be relocated to new buildings before demolition. Council asks about east campus future; told future phase with NYS, community engagement during construction period (~2+ years).

political_signal.description: Council pressing for transparency on east campus demolition plans and demanding community engagement comparable to East Adams project

processing_notes: Fire, Police, DPD in detected_departments are contextual references; actual department is Neighborhood and Business Development
HCR = New York State Homes and Community Renewal; HFA = Housing Finance Agency (subdivision of HCR)
Michael Collins = Commissioner for Neighborhood and Business Development (not in entity registry)
Building addresses: west campus = 1809, 1811, 1813, 1815, 1901, 1903; east campus = 2000-block
East campus tunnels between buildings = original mid-century design for fire access, now exploited for criminal activity
